does: This paper presents GASP, a black-box framework for automatically generating adversarial suffixes that jailbreak large language models by bypassing safety guardrails. The method leverages latent Bayesian optimization to explore continuous embedding spaces, avoiding discrete token optimization, and includes an iterative refinement procedure that balances attack efficacy with prompt coherence and naturalness. The core innovation is generating human-readable jailbreak prompts efficiently without requiring white-box access to model internals or gradient information, addressing limitations of prior manual heuristics and discrete optimization approaches that produce unnatural text or require prohibitive computational costs.
connects: This work builds on a growing literature on adversarial attacks against large language models, particularly jailbreak methods that elicit harmful outputs by circumventing safety alignment mechanisms. It responds to limitations in earlier approaches: manual heuristic-based jailbreaks lack generalizability, while gradient-based and discrete token optimization methods produce detectable artifacts or incur high computational overhead. The paper connects to broader research in adversarial machine learning and prompt injection attacks, as well as work on continuous optimization in discrete spaces through latent representations, drawing inspiration from techniques like variational autoencoders and Bayesian optimization for black-box settings.
enables: This research establishes foundations for understanding LLM vulnerabilities in realistic black-box threat scenarios where attackers lack model internals, informing development of more robust defenses. The latent space optimization approach could generalize to other discrete optimization problems in NLP security. Future work might explore detection mechanisms for naturally-appearing adversarial suffixes, investigate defenses against latent-space attacks, or extend the framework to multi-turn conversations and heterogeneous model families. The emphasis on prompt coherence opens avenues for studying the tradeoff between attack success and detectability, potentially motivating safer alignment techniques that withstand both automated and human-crafted adversarial prompts.
